name: rust-patterns
description: Evaluations for rust-patterns skill trigger accuracy

evals.name: trigger-positive-explicit
evals.description: Skill should trigger when user explicitly requests Rust best practices or patterns

prompts.input: How should I handle errors idiomatically in Rust?
prompts.reason: Rust idiomatic error handling with Result/? is a core topic of this skill

prompts.input: Help me design the ownership and borrowing for this Rust struct
prompts.reason: Ownership and borrowing are central to this skill

prompts.input: How do I use thiserror and anyhow in my Rust library?
prompts.reason: thiserror/anyhow error crates are explicitly covered in this skill

prompts.input: Refactor this Rust code to use idiomatic iterator chains instead of loops
prompts.reason: Prefer iterator chains over loops is a key Rust idiom in this skill

evals.name: trigger-negative-general
evals.description: Skill should NOT trigger for non-Rust or general programming requests

prompts.input: How do I manage memory in C++?
prompts.reason: C++ memory management, not Rust patterns

prompts.input: How do I install Rust on Windows?
prompts.reason: Installation question, not about Rust coding patterns

prompts.input: What is the difference between a thread and a process?
prompts.reason: General OS concept, not Rust-specific patterns
